method: Fresh verifier sub-agent (direct fetch of Moonshot's own kimi.com/blog/kimi-k3 announcement plus independent-outlet cross-check) and independent adversarial falsifier sub-agent, run sequentially with a tight time budget (deadline-constrained cycle).
origin_items: data/raw/2026-07-17/gnews-ai-model-releases.json and prior nights' gnews-ai-model-releases.json items on the Kimi K3 leak/launch

dedup-check run before opening: --title "Moonshot AI releases Kimi K3" — only a low-score (0.4) moderate match against the unrelated bytedance-edgebench-scaling-law story, no genuine conflict, cleared as standalone

claims.text: Moonshot AI launched Kimi K3 on 16 July 2026, with API access available immediately
claims.reasoning: Tier-0 self-report (kimi.com/blog/kimi-k3, fetched directly) confirms the launch and immediate API availability. Falsifier resolved an apparent date discrepancy (a July 14 leaked promo page vs. the live blog post) as leak-then-launch: the promo leaked July 14, actual launch landed July 16, with press (Axios, VentureBeat, Forbes, Tom's Hardware) independently reporting July 16-17. Independently bylined outlets confirm the fact of launch, not just Moonshot's own claim.

claims.text: Full model weights are due 27 July 2026; K3 is API-only until then, not yet an open-weight download
claims.reasoning: Stated directly on Moonshot's own blog and corroborated by press. This distinction matters editorially: falsifier flagged that calling K3 an 'open-weight model' in the present tense, before weights ship, would be premature framing -- article uses future/conditional framing ('once weights ship') throughout rather than present-tense open-weight claims.

claims.text: 2.8-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model, 16-of-896 experts active, 1M-token context window
claims.reasoning: All figures trace to Moonshot's own blog post; press (VentureBeat, Tom's Hardware, SCMP, SiliconANGLE) repeats the same numbers without independent verification -- classic independence collapse per verification.md, especially since weights (the only way to independently confirm parameter count) are not public until 27 July. Labeled vendor-claim, not confirmed.

claims.text: "Largest open-weight model" superlative and internal benchmark comparisons vs. rival models
claims.reasoning: Superlative traces to Moonshot's own comparison chart; falsifier found benchmark comparisons reportedly use different agent harnesses for Kimi vs. rivals (apples-to-oranges), and no independent, same-harness reproduction exists yet. Kept as vendor-claim, attributed explicitly to Moonshot and press repetition, not stated as fact.

notes: Adversarial falsifier pass explicitly recommended PUBLISH with narrow framing: attribute all specs/benchmarks/superlatives to Moonshot, avoid unqualified present-tense 'open-weight' framing until 27 July weights ship, and omit the separate/unrelated 2026-02 Anthropic-Moonshot distillation dispute as a conflation risk out of scope for a release story (omitted from the article). Exact API pricing was left out of the article entirely: sources disagreed on the precise figure (draft brief cited $0.30/MTok cached input vs. VentureBeat's $3/$15 per MTok) and reconciling it was not load-bearing for the story, so it was dropped rather than published unreconciled. Anthropic-Moonshot distillation history noted here for future reference only, not included in the article.
